content.full_text:
October 5, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

To whom it may concern:

I spoke to your consultant Colleen Mickey on October 3, 1991. The question I asked was whether the newsletter we printed was to be reported on form 635 when it contained articles encouraging Mountain Lion Foundation donors to contact their legislators on important votes.

The answer I received was that if we print a newsletter asking our members to vote for a proposition, we are required to file a campaign statement as either an inkind independent contributor or inkind contributor. Also the total cost of the newsletter is to be reported as the amount of the contribution regardless of how much of the newsletter was actually dedicated to the proposition or measure.

If we ask our members to write to their legislator regarding specific legislation, we would then need to note this amount on the form 635 and the same rule on dollar amounts apply as answered above.

Please send us confirmation in writing of the above. I have enclosed copies of our lobbying filings and our newsletter. This newsletter is published twice a year.

Thank you,

Carsynn Costa
Administrative Coordinator
MOUNTAIN LION FOUNDATION

The image is completely blank, showing only a plain white background with no visible text, images, or markings.
